description: Landscape-scale regions defined by terrain and geography.
regions: Borderlands
Contested frontier where rival banners flutter and no traveler sleeps without a watch.
Heartland
The fertile, well-settled core of a realm, far from danger and rich with harvests.
Hinterland
Remote backcountry seldom visited by merchants, where old ways linger undisturbed.
Lowlands
Broad, flat country of river-fed fields and slow canals, often shrouded in morning mist.
Highlands
Rugged, wind-scoured uplands of thin soil and proud clans who bow to no lowland king.
Midlands
Gently rolling country between mountain and coast, a patchwork of farms and market towns.
Outlands
Distant frontier where maps grow vague and the next settlement is always a day too far.
Wastelands
Blighted, lifeless terrain where nothing thrives and even the wind sounds hollow.
Grasslands
An endless sea of waist-high grass bending under a constant wind, hiding horizon after horizon.
Moorlands
Open, treeless upland of heather and bog, swept by rain and threaded with sheep trails.
Drylands
Parched earth cracked under a merciless sun, where water is worth more than gold.
Coastlands
A windblown strip where salt spray meets plowed earth and fishing villages dot the cliffs.
Basin
A broad depression ringed by higher ground, gathering rivers and settlements at its lowest point.
Vale
A shallow valley of gentle streams and wildflower meadows sheltered by low ridges.
Dale
A wide, sunlit valley with a slow river at its heart and farms climbing the slopes.
Glen
A tight, shadowed valley where streams echo off close stone walls.
Taiga
Endless dark spruce stretching to the frozen north, silent save for wind and distant wolves.
Savanna
Sun-baked grassland dotted with flat-topped trees, alive with herds and the shimmer of heat.
Expanse
A vast, featureless sweep of land where sky and earth blur into a single pale line.
Reach
A long arm of territory stretching outward, connecting heartland to some distant resource or border.
Span
A measured stretch of land linking two landmarks, named for the journey between them.
Stretch
A continuous run of similar terrain, monotonous to cross but vital to hold.
Tract
A defined parcel of land set aside for a particular use, be it timber, grazing, or exile.
Swathe
A broad band of territory cut through different terrain, often following a river or ancient road.
Belt
A narrow ribbon of distinct land running between two unlike regions.
Heath
Open, uncultivated ground of gorse and bracken, lonely and exposed to every sky.
Scrubland
Low, thorny brush clinging to dry soil, offering neither shade nor easy passage.
Brushland
Dense, tangled growth of woody shrubs that snag clothing and slow every step.
